title: Parameria glandulifera
taxonomy: Parameria glandulifera is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Parameria, which is a group of plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. Within the Rubiaceae family, Parameria glandulifera is further divided into its own unique classification. The exact classification of Parameria glandulifera is: Parameria glandulifera (Hook.f.) P.D.Bantah. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic makeup.

intro: Parameria glandulifera, also known as Parameria, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. It is a shrub native to tropical regions, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it beneficial in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Parameria glandulifera include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Parameria glandulifera include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory problems.
